titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Emmanuel Zeid New Haven Slide Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by William E. Brown, Jr. and Georgia Esau

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:03

did.unittitle: Emanuel Zeid New Haven slide collection
did.abstract: The collection consists of 35mm film slides (color and black and white) relating to New Haven area buildings, sites, and people, 1950-1979, with an emphasis on urban redevelopment projects, construction, and political events. A group of slides relating to New England log homes is also included.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Deposited by Sylvia Zeid, 1984. Gift of Sylvia Zeid, 1985.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Emanuel Zeid New Haven Slide Collection (MS 1391). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The 7,000 slides are arranged in the collection by subject area.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of 35mm film slides (color and black and white) relating to New Haven area buildings, sites, and people, 1950-1979, with an emphasis on urban redevelopment projects, construction, and political events. A group of slides relating to New England log homes is also included.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: New Haven aerial views: tower, 135 slides
New Haven Festivals on Green, 82 slides
New Haven, Wooster Street, Fair Haven, East Haven, West Haven, 129 slides
